title: Love in the Time of Cholera
author: Gabriel García Márquez

setting.note: García Márquez never names the Caribbean port of the novel, but readers and scholars identify it as Cartagena de Indias, with touches of Barranquilla and the Magdalena River. The walled colonial city, its cathedral and its seafront arcades supply the streets where Florentino Ariza waits half a century for Fermina Daza.

palette.note: A deep Caribbean rose for a fifty-year love. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper at 6.46:1.
